channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌏 US Army integrates veterinarians into human combat care | Defense News
item.description: The Army wants to better utilize veterinarians in combat medicine to prepare for the possibility of large-scale operations in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: 📍 Washington Shouldn’t Fly Solo on Building Space Superiority | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2025, Nazmelis Zengin wrote, “The Fragility of U.S. Space Power in a Multipolar World,” where she argued Washington’s space superiority could be challenged if the United States doesn’t rethink its course, taking lessons from mid-tier space powers. A year later, we asked…

item.title: 🌏 F/A-18 Super Hornets Eyed To Replace Navy’s Remaining F-5 Adversaries | The War Zone
item.description: Swapping dated F-5s for F/A-18E/Fs would give the Navy a more threat representative adversary fleet to emulate advanced enemies, like China. The post F/A-18 Super Hornets Eyed To Replace Navy’s Remaining F-5 Adversaries appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ US Army receives first lightweight Javelin launchers | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. Army has received its first batch of new, lighter Javelin launchers, defense contractor RTX announced in a statement Tuesday.

item.title: 🌍 After Iran attacks, Turkish industry seeing increased Gulf interest in air defense platforms | Breaking Defense
item.description: Both companies expressed their readiness to transfer technology in line with mandates in Gulf states to localize defense production.

item.title: 📍 Don’t call it a G7 comeback | Atlantic Council
item.description: The G7’s equity rebound may look broad-based. In reality, it’s largely a tale of US tech dominance, driven by the “Magnificent Seven” and an extraordinary concentration of global market power. The post Don’t call it a G7 comeback appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 How the US, allies and adversaries see the future of drone wingmen | Breaking Defense
item.description: In the third video focused on manned-unmanned teaming, we trace how the concept has evolved inside the US defense industry and among other militaries across the globe.

item.title: 🕵️ Defense-driven demand powers surge in US listings by mining firms | Defense News
item.description: This year’s transactions have brought producers of antimony, rare earths, tungsten and uranium to the NYSE and Nasdaq.

item.title: 🏴 Senior Ukrainian commander sees imminent ‘turning point’ in war | Defense News
item.description: Ukraine has a six-month window in which to seize the battlefield initiative from Russia and strengthen its hand for peace talks, a senior commander said.

item.title: 📍 Canada enters talks with Saab for GlobalEye purchase | Breaking Defense
item.description: While no deal is finalized, Sweden’s Prime Minister stated that he looks forward to “welcoming Canada into the GlobalEye family.”

item.title: 🌍 Kroenig interviewed on NPR on Abraham Accords and Iran | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 27, Atlantic Council vice president and Scowcroft Center senior director Matthew Kroenig was interviewed on NPR, arguing that President Trump is pushing for more countries to join the Abraham Accords to help secure a viable deal with Iran. The post Kroenig interviewed on…

item.title: 🏴 Canada to buy Polish drones, deepen defense ties by leveraging EU SAFE funds | Breaking Defense
item.description: In December, Canada became the only non-European country to gain entry into the €150 billion SAFE defense borrowing and procurement scheme.

item.title: 🌍 Inside the Struggle to Build an Iranian Opposition | Foreign Policy
item.description: A new group is trying to create what its predecessors could not: an effective democratic force for Iran.

item.title: 🌍 The KC-46 has finally landed in Israel. The US needs to keep pushing forward. | Breaking Defense
item.description: Israel now has its own KC-46, but the US needs to do much more to help grow Israel’s tanker force, argue Justin Leopold-Cohen and Bradley Bowman.

item.title: 🛡️ Shooting Down Civil Aircraft: What International and U.S. Law Say About a Charge in the Raul Castro Indictment | Just Security
item.description: Q&A providing an overview of the legal framework governing shootdowns, including their status under both international and domestic U.S. law The post Shooting Down Civil Aircraft: What International and U.S. Law Say About a Charge in the Raul Castro Indictment appeared first on…

item.title: 📍 I Was Afghanistan’s Attorney General. Here Is What Justice Looked Like — and What Destroyed It. | Just Security
item.description: Afghanistan’s justice system took 20 years to build and 11 days to destroy. Former Attorney General Mohammad Farid Hamidi outlines the ongoing fight for accountability. The post I Was Afghanistan’s Attorney General. Here Is What Justice Looked Like — and What Destroyed It.…

item.title: 📍 How the Twenty-Fifth Amendment Applies Today | Just Security
item.description: Yale Law School's Peter Gruber Rule of Law Clinic has updated its Reader's Guide to the 25th Amendment to cover Trump and Biden-era developments. The post How the Twenty-Fifth Amendment Applies Today appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 The sights of Turkey’s Efes military drill | Breaking Defense
item.description: Breaking Defense was on the ground for Turkey’s massive combined forces drill Efes 2026, which brought ships, drones, air defense and helicopters together.

item.title: 🌍 Kroenig quoted in the Christian Science Monitor on Iran | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 18, Atlantic Council vice president and Scowcroft Center senior director Matthew Kroenig was quoted in the Christian Science Monitor on how Rubio entered Trump's inner circle. The post Kroenig quoted in the Christian Science Monitor on Iran appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 📍 International Accountability for U.S. Crimes in the Caribbean and Pacific | Just Security
item.description: If no one acts, U.S. crimes in the Caribbean and Pacific will embolden other bad actors and encourage more violations of international law. The post International Accountability for U.S. Crimes in the Caribbean and Pacific appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 Edge computing fails when the mission hardware can’t evolve | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] The payoff is quicker tech insertion, avoiding vendor lock, and more room for autonomy, sensors, weapons, and mission growth.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: May 27, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said yesterday that it would launch a “decisive reciprocal…

item.title: 📍 Collection: U.S. Lethal Strikes on Suspected Drug Traffickers, Operation Southern Spear, Operation Absolute Resolve | Just Security
item.description: Collection of expert analysis on the legality of the U.S. strike on Venezuelan vessels in the Caribbean, the consequences of the strike, and related issues. The post Collection: U.S. Lethal Strikes on Suspected Drug Traffickers, Operation Southern Spear, Operation Absolute…

item.title: 📍 The ‘Lost’ Villages of Myanmar’s Rakhine | Bellingcat
item.description: A “river of blood” was how one survivor described the scene in western Myanmar. “I saw shooting. I saw mass killing.” Another told the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights (UNHRC) how 20 relatives, including three children, had been killed in the 2024 attack on Htan Shauk Khan…

item.title: 📍 On AI, It’s the Pope vs. Trump | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Vatican has made a frontal challenge to the Trump administration’s approach to technology.

item.title: 🏴 Latvia sends mobile intercept units to Russian border in wake of drone incursions | Defense News
item.description: “The force teams we plan to deploy to the border in the coming days will be equipped with local interceptor drones,” a Latvian military official said.

item.title: 🔐 Synthetic Biology, Drones, and AI: The Risks of Dual-Use Technologies | War on the Rocks
item.description: Is it too late to stop criminals and American adversaries from exploiting AI to conduct cyberattacks or design novel pathogens? Has regulation kept pace with the threat civilian drones pose to critical infrastructure? AI researcher Lennart Heim, Army drone strategist Paul…

item.title: 📍 The Road to Space Runs through the Poles | War on the Rocks
item.description: Why are there more antennas on Svalbard than anywhere else on Earth? Svalbard of all places, where cats and childbirth are banned and there are more polar bears than people? This cluster of islands in the Arctic, one thousand kilometers from Norway, is key to everything from…

item.title: 🏛️ Canadian fighter pilot school to get M-346 trainer jets from Leonardo | Defense News
item.description: The ITPS currently trains pilots on the Aero Vodochody L-39 and is working with over ten air forces to provide advanced jet training, the school CEO said.

item.title: 🏛️ Leading in the Dark: How Submarine Commanders Think Under Uncertainty | War on the Rocks
item.description: We had been tracking the contact for six hours.The acoustic signature was ambiguous. The geometry was incomplete. The tactical picture had shifted twice in the preceding hour.I ordered battle stations anyway. Not because I was certain, I was not. I ordered it because the…
